title: Digital Divide & Learning
description: What is the impact of the digital divide on student performance during prolonged remote learning periods?
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed studies, surveys, and case reports that quantify how disparities in internet access, device ownership, and digital literacy affect academic outcomes, attendance, and mental health among students from diverse socio‑economic backgrounds during extended periods of remote instruction in the U.S., Europe, and Asia.

title: Sustainable Restaurant Options
description: Where can I find eco-friendly dining venues in central Cambridge that offer plant‑based menus and reduce plastic waste?
narrative: I need recommendations for restaurants that prioritize sustainability through zero‑waste policies, locally sourced ingredients, and vegan options, specifically near the university campus. Relevance requires evidence of environmental initiatives, menu composition, and proximity.

title: AI Ethics in Medicine
description: What are the key ethical concerns associated with using AI algorithms in clinical diagnosis?
narrative: The user is looking for a comprehensive overview of the ethical issues that arise when deploying AI-driven diagnostic tools in healthcare settings, such as algorithmic bias, lack of transparency, patient consent, privacy, and liability. Relevant documents should contain academic research, policy analysis, and practical case studies that focus specifically on AI in medical diagnostics, rather than generic AI ethics or unrelated medical topics.

title: Sustainable Agriculture Policies
description: Which countries have the most effective sustainable agriculture policies and what measurable outcomes have they achieved?
narrative: The user wants to identify national policies that promote sustainable agriculture, focusing on policy types, implementation timelines, and quantifiable impacts on yield, biodiversity, and economic resilience. Relevant documents should provide policy descriptions, case studies, comparative analyses, and metrics that demonstrate tangible outcomes.

title: Senior Home Workouts
description: What low‑impact indoor workouts are best for seniors with knee pain?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based exercise routines that can be performed at home, involve minimal equipment, reduce knee strain, and include safety tips and progression guidelines.

title: Satellite Glacial Data
description: Where can I find recent satellite-derived graphs indicating glacial retreat across the Andes?
narrative: The user is researching climate change impacts and requires up-to-date, high-resolution satellite imagery or data visualizations depicting the extent of glacial retreat in the Andes region over the past decade. Relevant documents should include reputable sources such as NASA, ESA, or peer‑reviewed journals, provide time‑stamped maps or charts, and include geographical coordinates or extent metrics. Documents that are outdated (pre‑2000), lack source credibility, or only contain text descriptions without visual data should be considered irrelevant.

title: Visa Rental Offers
description: I want to compare the discount percentages and eligibility rules for each Visa card level when renting a car in France.
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on how each Visa tier (Classic, Premier, Platinum, Infinite, Business variants) affects car rental discounts, including the specific vehicle categories covered, duration limits, and geographic restrictions, so that they can choose the most advantageous plan for a planned trip.

title: Rainwater Harvesting Ordinances
description: Which U.S. city councils have passed ordinances incentivizing residential rainwater harvesting systems?
narrative: The user wants a list of municipalities, details of the incentives (tax credits, rebates, permits), and any documented outcomes or case studies; information should be current up to 2025.

title: Urban Food Deserts
description: What are proven strategies for reducing food insecurity in urban food‑desert neighborhoods?
narrative: The user is a policy analyst researching actionable interventions to alleviate food insecurity in cities that lack reliable access to affordable, nutritious food. Relevance requires evidence‑based studies, pilot program reports, municipal policy documents, and comparative analyses that detail implementation mechanisms, cost‑effectiveness, and measurable outcomes. Documents that merely list policy names or provide general commentary without specific strategy details or outcome data are considered insufficient.

title: AI Regulation Data
description: What publicly available datasets and documents detail cross‑border AI regulatory compliance frameworks for European and US markets?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, up‑to‑date sources that enumerate specific regulations, compliance guidelines, and cross‑border interactions for AI systems. Relevant items should include regulatory texts, comparison tables, case studies, or official reports from EU or US authorities that provide actionable insight into compliance requirements.

title: Antimicrobial Resistance Policy
description: In what ways can national governments integrate antimicrobial stewardship into public health policy to effectively curb antimicrobial resistance?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on the design and implementation of governmental strategies and regulatory frameworks that promote antimicrobial stewardship, including case studies from multiple countries, evidence-based interventions, and legislative measures. Relevance criteria: sources must discuss policy mechanisms, stewardship programs, and public health governance; general consumer advisories or clinical guidelines not tied to national policy are excluded.

title: Smart Hydroponic Sensors
description: How can I select, install, and integrate smart sensor technologies into a home hydroponic system to optimize plant growth and resource usage?
narrative: The user seeks practical, detailed guidance on choosing, mounting, calibrating, and interpreting data from sensors measuring nutrient concentration, pH, temperature, humidity, and light intensity for a small‑scale hydroponic garden. Relevance criteria include real‑world implementation case studies, vendor specifications, programming or firmware integration steps, and performance evaluation. Generic introductions to hydroponics or sensor overviews without actionable integration details are not considered relevant.

title: Syncretic Religious Practices
description: What evidence exists of religious syncretism between ancient Mesopotamian and neighboring cultures during the first millennium BCE?
narrative: The user seeks scholarly discussions, archaeological findings, and textual analyses that demonstrate the blending or borrowing of deities, myths, and rituals across cultures such as Sumerian, Akkadian, Babylonian, Hurrian, and Elamite. Relevance requires peer‑reviewed articles, primary source translations, and comparative studies published within the last two decades.

title: ASEAN Climate Policies
description: Which climate policy frameworks have the ASEAN member states adopted between 2015 and 2020?
narrative: An analyst seeks to compare the official climate policy frameworks of ASEAN member countries over the period 2015‑2020. Relevance is determined by documents that disclose national commitments, legislative or regulatory frameworks, policy briefs, or governmental reports issued during or shortly after that window. The focus is on policy documents rather than implementation studies, news reports, or academic analyses.

title: Privacy versus Utility
description: How can clinicians balance protecting patient privacy with the need for comprehensive data to improve diagnostics?
narrative: The user seeks guidance on protocols, de‑identification standards, differential privacy, and governance models to ensure both privacy compliance and analytic value. Relevance criteria include discussions on HIPAA, GDPR, privacy‑preserving analytics, and case studies illustrating the trade‑off between data utility and patient privacy.

title: Text‑Driven Site Reinterpretation
description: Which archaeological sites worldwide have had their official interpretations revised because of textual evidence?
narrative: The user is seeking documented examples of sites where scholars have altered the accepted archaeological understanding after discovering or re‑analyzing contemporary texts (e.g., histories, inscriptions, literary references). Relevance is restricted to cases where the revised interpretation was explicitly linked to textual data, was published in peer‑reviewed or reputable sources, and led to a formal shift in the site's accepted identity, chronology, or function. The user wants at least one example per major cultural area, with clear evidence of the textual–archaeological interplay.

title: Digital Nomad Visa
description: What are the requirements and application steps for obtaining a digital nomad visa in Estonia?
narrative: I am a software engineer planning to relocate to Estonia to work remotely and need detailed, up‑to‑date information on eligibility criteria, required documentation, visa duration, and any tax or health insurance obligations. Relevant documents should come from official Estonian government or embassy sources, recent policy updates, and reputable travel‑legal advisories.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are the primary environmental factors contributing to urban heat islands in megacities?
narrative: The user wants a technical, data‑driven explanation that goes beyond surface‑level descriptions. Relevant sources should discuss anthropogenic heat, surface albedo, building geometry, urban vegetation, and policy responses, with empirical studies, modelling results, or evidence‑based policy analyses. Documents that merely define the term, provide general climate‑change overviews, or focus on non‑urban heat regimes should be considered irrelevant.

title: Urban Building Sustainability
description: What are the comparative sustainability metrics of high‑rise buildings versus mixed‑use developments in major U.S. cities?
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed studies, industry reports, and data sets that directly compare energy efficiency, carbon footprints, water usage, and life‑cycle assessment results for high‑rise and mixed‑use structures. Relevance requires documents that present measurable metrics, case studies or comparative analyses, while excluding generic marketing material or unrelated architectural listings.

title: Data Privacy for SMEs
description: What are the key requirements and best practices for small and medium‑sized enterprises to comply with the EU General Data Protection Regulation while maintaining operational efficiency?
narrative: The user needs detailed, actionable guidance on GDPR compliance tailored to SMEs, covering cost‑effective tools, common pitfalls, legal responsibilities, audit preparation, and recent case studies or practical checklists.

title: Climate Finance Impact
description: What are the measurable effects of climate finance on the resilience of coastal communities in the Caribbean?
narrative: The user seeks to understand how funds allocated to climate adaptation have altered the capacity of Caribbean coastal villages to withstand extreme weather events. Relevance requires studies that report specific impacts on infrastructure, livelihoods, biodiversity, and socioeconomic indicators, focusing on projects financed by international climate funds (e.g., Green Climate Fund, World Bank) during 2010–2024.

title: Mindfulness Stress Management
description: What evidence exists on the effectiveness of mindfulness-based interventions for reducing perceived stress in corporate employees?
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed systematic reviews, meta‑analyses, and randomized controlled trials published from 2015 onward that report effect sizes for perceived stress, physiological stress markers, or employee well‑being outcomes in workplace settings.

title: Rural Renewable Initiatives
description: What recent projects have implemented renewable energy solutions in rural areas of sub‑Saharan Africa, and what were their outcomes?
narrative: The user is a policy analyst researching effective models for electrification in low‑income rural settings. They seek case studies of solar PV, mini‑grid, and micro‑hydro deployments that include details on project scale, funding sources, implementation partners, and measurable impacts (such as hours of electricity per household, cost per kWh, and socio‑economic benefits). Relevance is judged by the geographic focus on sub‑Saharan Africa, the renewable nature of the energy source, the rural context, evidence of project completion, and documented outcome data.

title: Heritage Sustainability Angers
description: How can I visit Angers' historic sites sustainably without renting a car?
narrative: The user seeks low‑carbon travel options to explore Angers’ historical attractions, prioritizing public transit, bike rentals, walking routes, and eco‑friendly tours. Irrelevant documents that focus solely on car rentals or high‑emission transport are not relevant. The answer should emphasize availability, cost, convenience, and environmental impact of alternatives for a short visit.

title: Organic Potato Market
description: What factors have driven growth in the organic potato sector over the past decade?
narrative: The user is looking for a detailed, data‑driven analysis of the organic potato market, including supply chain dynamics, consumer behavior, regulatory changes, pricing trends, and geographic demand patterns. Relevance requires documents that provide recent market research, industry reports, academic studies, or policy analyses explicitly addressing the organic potato segment; general agricultural background information, unrelated crop studies, or purely descriptive histories should not be considered relevant because they do not address the specific market‑focused needs of the inquiry.

title: Village Terroir Comparison
description: What are the distinguishing terroir characteristics and wine styles among Burgundy and Rhône villages?
narrative: The user wants an in‑depth comparison of how terroir shapes wine styles across various French wine villages, especially in Burgundy, Rhône, and Bordeaux. Relevance criteria therefore require sources that describe climate, soil, vineyard practices, grape varieties, and typical flavor profiles for each village, including scholarly articles, regional wine guides, and comparative studies. Pure product listings, commercial promotions, or generic wine descriptions are not considered relevant.

title: Urban Micro-Insurance Impact
description: What measurable social and economic effects does micro-insurance have on households living in informal urban settlements over the last decade?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date, peer‑reviewed case studies and empirical data on how micro‑insurance programs influence income stability, health outcomes, and risk management among urban slum residents, prioritizing studies from 2015 onward and excluding anecdotal reports.

title: Cybersecurity Trends 2025
description: What are the emerging cybersecurity trends expected to shape the industry in 2025?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of upcoming cybersecurity practices, threat landscapes, and policy developments relevant to IT professionals, regulators, and businesses, focusing on innovations, major vulnerabilities, and compliance requirements that will influence security strategies in 2025.

title: Stroke Rehabilitation Outcomes
description: What factors most influence long‑term functional recovery in adults following ischemic stroke?
narrative: The user seeks evidence‑based determinants of post‑stroke recovery to inform prediction models of functional independence. Relevant documents should present empirical research, meta‑analyses, or systematic reviews that quantify how demographic, clinical, and intervention variables (e.g., age, lesion location, early mobilization, cognitive status) affect outcomes such as the modified Rankin Scale or the Barthel Index at 6–12 months after stroke.

title: Green School Certification
description: Which green building certification standards are most applicable to public elementary schools, and how do they affect operating costs and student health outcomes?
narrative: A district administrator seeks to evaluate sustainable building options for new school construction. Relevance criteria include detailed certification requirements (LEED, WELL, Green Globes), case studies of schools using each standard, quantified impacts on utilities and maintenance budgets, and empirical evidence linking certification to indoor air quality, learning environment, and student performance. The user needs concise, actionable comparisons to support budgeting and policy decisions.

title: Urban AV Policy
description: Which regulatory frameworks and policy measures are essential to safely integrate autonomous vehicles into existing urban traffic systems, and how have different cities approached these challenges?
narrative: The user is a city planner tasked with drafting a comprehensive policy for autonomous vehicle (AV) deployment. They want detailed evidence of regulatory approaches, safety and liability standards, data‑privacy safeguards, infrastructure modifications, and cost‑benefit analyses from multiple international case studies. Relevance criteria include official policy documents, legislative texts, and peer‑reviewed implementation studies published between 2018 and 2025, with clear metrics on safety incidents, traffic flow improvements, and public acceptance. The user prioritizes recent, empirically supported information that can be adapted to their municipal context.

title: Remote Work Traffic Shift
description: What are the measurable effects of increased remote work on urban traffic congestion and public transportation usage?
narrative: The user aims to understand how the rise in remote work patterns influences traffic volume, congestion levels, and the demand for public transit in large metropolitan areas. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, studies, or reports that quantify changes in traffic counts, peak hour loads, ride‑share usage, and transit ridership before and after remote work adoption.

title: Smart City Energy Efficiency
description: What are the most effective strategies and technologies for improving energy efficiency in existing smart city infrastructure?
narrative: The user seeks research-based insights, case studies, and best‑practice guidelines that identify actionable techniques—such as adaptive lighting, HVAC optimization, and waste‑heat recovery—along with their implementation challenges and cost‑benefit trade‑offs, to guide decisions for existing smart city projects.

title: Ethical AI Translation
description: What are the key ethical considerations when developing AI systems that translate between multiple natural languages?
narrative: The user seeks peer‑reviewed articles, guidelines, and case studies that discuss cultural bias, data privacy, and equitable representation in multilingual AI translation systems. Relevance is judged by the presence of comprehensive ethical frameworks, practical examples, and up‑to‑date scholarly references.

title: Intonation for Speakers
description: What evidence‑based techniques can help non‑native speakers improve their intonation and natural prosody when giving public presentations?
narrative: The user is a non‑native English speaker who regularly presents in academic settings and feels their speech is monotonous. They seek practical, scientifically grounded methods for pitch variation, rhythm, and breath control, including lesson plans, audio examples, or workshops that can be applied directly to public speaking contexts.

title: GPU‑Accelerated Sparse Matrix Libraries
description: Which high‑performance sparse matrix libraries provide GPU acceleration suitable for deep‑learning workloads?
narrative: The user is a software engineer looking to integrate efficient sparse matrix support into a machine‑learning pipeline. The desired solutions must offer GPU off‑loading, Python bindings, active maintenance, clear performance benchmarks, and permissive licensing. Relevance is judged by documented GPU support, benchmark results on modern GPUs, open‑source community activity, and ease of integration with popular ML frameworks.

title: Legacy Hi‑Fi Streaming
description: Which older home audio receivers can be retrofitted to support modern streaming services such as Spotify, Apple Music, and Tidal?
narrative: The user is a audiophile who owns a vintage 120‑W receiver but wants to add streaming capabilities without replacing the entire system. Relevant documents should describe retrofit solutions (e.g., streaming adapters, network interfaces, firmware updates) that integrate into existing receivers, provide connections to streaming services via Wi‑Fi, Bluetooth or Apple AirPlay, and preserve the original sound quality.

title: Vinyl Exchange Sites
description: What online platforms allow collectors to buy, sell, or trade high‑grade vinyl records?
narrative: The user is an enthusiast of rare and high‑grade vinyl looking for community‑driven marketplaces or forums where safe transactions and expert grading are emphasized. Relevant documents should detail specific sites, user experiences, shipping practices, authenticity verification, and privacy policies tailored to vinyl collectors.

title: AI Ethics Hiring
description: What are the ethical concerns and best practices for using AI in the hiring process?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative sources that explain the ethical risks, impact on bias, legal considerations, and recommended guidelines for using AI tools in recruiting and selection. Relevant material includes empirical studies, white papers, legal analyses, and industry case studies that address bias, fairness, transparency, and compliance.

title: AI Mental Diagnostics
description: What ethical concerns and diagnostic accuracy issues arise when applying AI tools for adolescent depression detection?
narrative: The researcher is looking for comprehensive evidence on algorithmic fairness, data privacy, informed consent, and performance metrics related to AI systems diagnosing depression in teenagers. Relevant documents should include peer‑reviewed studies, systematic reviews, conference papers, and industry guidelines that discuss bias, sensitivity/specificity, validation protocols, and regulatory or ethical frameworks.

title: Green Building Certifications
description: Which green building certification is most widely recognized for commercial office buildings in North America, and what core assessment criteria do they each examine?
narrative: The user seeks to compare major green building certification programs—LEED, BREEAM, WELL, and Green Globes—to determine which holds the greatest industry recognition and which core environmental, health, and economic factors each awards high scores on. Relevance criteria: documents that discuss the recognition, market penetration, assessment criteria sections, scoring frameworks, or case studies of commercial office buildings implementing these certificates are considered relevant.

title: Vegan Pastry Certification
description: What steps and requirements are needed to obtain EU certification for a bakery as vegan-only?
narrative: The user seeks detailed guidance on the regulatory pathway, required documentation, ingredient compliance, and audit processes specific to EU regions for bakeries that exclusively produce vegan pastries. Relevance criteria include up-to-date legal references, checklists for suppliers, and case studies of successful certification claims.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What are the most effective strategies for reducing urban heat islands in mid-sized cities?
narrative: The user seeks recent, evidence-based recommendations and case studies on interventions such as green roofs, reflective pavements, and tree canopy, with relevance restricted to peer-reviewed research or official city planning documents published in the last five years.

title: Intermittent Fasting Cognitive Effects
description: Does 16/8 intermittent fasting improve cognitive performance in adults aged 45 to 60?
narrative: The user is a health researcher seeking peer-reviewed evidence on neurocognitive outcomes in middle-aged adults comparing intermittent fasting to regular meal patterns. Relevant documents must be published within the last 10 years, include at least 50 participants, and report measurable outcomes such as reaction time, memory tests, and executive function. Preferred sources are MEDLINE, Embase, and Cochrane reviews. Exclude animal studies, case reports, or non-English publications.

title: VR Early Learning
description: What evidence exists about the impact of virtual reality on cognitive development in children aged 4‑6?
narrative: The user is searching for peer‑reviewed studies that demonstrate measurable cognitive gains, engagement metrics, or developmental concerns associated with VR use in early childhood. Documents must be empirical, involve controlled or longitudinal designs, and focus on children aged 4–6; stand‑alone news articles, opinion pieces, or general product reviews are not relevant.
